results.finalAnswer:
ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Components/Api/Application/Services/ApplicationService.php - central request-to-domain orchestration; create/update and prefill flows land here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Components/Api/Application/Repositories/ApplicationRepository.php - persistence choke point for application queries and saves that request changes are likely to affect
SUBSYSTEM: tests/CreatesApplication.php - test bootstrap for the Laravel kernel, so it is the safest place to verify request-path regressions

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: routes/api_v1.php - main API route map, including `/applications` and endpoint-specific middleware
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - global and `api` middleware stack for auth, throttling, logging, and route bindings
SUBSYSTEM: app/Components/Api/Application/Services/ApplicationService.php - central application write flow used by create/update/prefill, where payload changes take effect

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Components/Api/Application/Services/ApplicationService.php - likely main request-to-business-logic handoff for application flows
SUBSYSTEM: app/Components/Api/Domain/DomainRepository.php - high-fan-in repository hotspot where safe query/persistence changes should be isolated
SUBSYSTEM: database/migrations - schema source of truth for validating request fields, constraints, and downstream DB impact

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - maps /api, /api/v2, and /api/v3 to the versioned route files and defines request rate limiters
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - controls global and route middleware, including auth, throttling, logging, and middleware execution order
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - safest boundary for endpoint-specific validation and authorization; controllers already inject FormRequests instead of embedding request rules

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - global and API middleware order lives here, including auth, throttling, logging, CORS, and request preprocessing
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - mounts the versioned API route files and defines named rate limiters, so it controls dispatch into v1/v2/v3
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - FormRequest validation/authorization layer; safest place to tighten or reshape incoming payloads before controllers and workflow handlers run

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - global and `api` middleware order is defined here, including auth setup, throttling, Sentry context, and request logging
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - mounts the versioned API route files and defines request rate-limit behavior, so route-level handling changes fan out from here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - FormRequest authorization/validation is the safest seam for changing request shape; some requests also switch to custom keyed validation errors via `WithValidationKeys`
